content: By 2025, autonomous self-supervised agents will evolve a hierarchical 'curiosity memory' architecture where intrinsic curiosity mechanisms (ICM/RND) prune and prioritize which unlabeled environmental observations get stored into long-term agent memory, rather than passively encoding all available data. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where the agent's own past curiosities shape the distribution of data it learns from next, eliminating the need for external curation of training datasets.
